message:
feat: expand FTS5 extension, add MVCC concurrent-writer and time-travel modules, extend pager

Major progress across four crates in the from-scratch SQLite implementation:

fsqlite-ext-fts5 (+2400 lines):
  Substantially expand the FTS5 full-text search extension with tokenizer
  infrastructure, BM25 ranking, prefix queries, column filters, boolean
  operators (AND/OR/NOT/NEAR), phrase matching, and the auxiliary function
  API. Adds the segment merge scheduler and incremental index maintenance
  (automerge, optimize, rebuild commands).

fsqlite-mvcc — 3 new modules (2559 lines total):
  - begin_concurrent.rs (§12.10): MVCC concurrent-writer mode where
    multiple transactions write simultaneously to different pages with
    first-committer-wins page-level conflict detection
  - time_travel.rs (§12.17): FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF support for reading
    historical database state via the immutable commit stream
  - two_phase_commit.rs (§12.11): cross-database 2PC protocol ensuring
    atomicity across multiple attached WAL-mode databases
  Also wires new modules into lib.rs with feature flags.

fsqlite-pager (+401 lines):
  Extend the pager with WAL-mode checkpoint logic, page cache eviction
  improvements, and new trait surface in traits.rs (+51 lines) for
  abstracted page-store operations.

fsqlite-types/cx.rs: add execution context extensions.
fsqlite-vfs/unix.rs: add VFS-level file operation hooks.
Cargo.lock: dependency updates for new fts5 dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  "fsqlite-error",

  "fsqlite-types",
+ "tracing",
 ]
